entry.title: 360username
entry.description: 360username provides a free username availability checker across multiple social media and domain platforms.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited free checks for username availability across supported platforms.

useCases: Check username availability for new projects
Research domain and social handle options
quickstartSteps: Go to https://360username.com/
Enter your desired username in the search box.
Review availability results across platforms.
Register available usernames or domains as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to quickly check if a username is available across social networks and domains before registering.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require automated or API-based username checks, or need bulk search capabilities.
entry.body: 360username is a free tool for checking username availability across social media and domain platforms, ideal for individuals and small teams seeking consistent branding.

entry.title: Abby
entry.description: Abby provides simple, no-code Stripe subscription billing and customer portal tools for SaaS and indie developers.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to $1,000 in processed revenue per month with all core features.
caveats: Abby branding on customer portal in free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Abby account.
Connect your Stripe account.
Configure your products and pricing in Abby.
Embed the customer portal link in your app or website.
entry.whenToUse: Use Abby when you need to launch Stripe-based subscription billing and a customer portal without writing backend code.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced billing logic, custom checkout flows, or need to remove Abby branding on the free tier.
entry.body: Abby is a no-code Stripe billing and customer portal solution designed for SaaS and indie developers to launch subscriptions quickly without backend complexity.

entry.title: Algolia
entry.description: Algolia provides a hosted search API for fast, relevant, and scalable search experiences in web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: The Algolia Community plan offers a free tier with limited indexing and operations for personal or open source projects.
caveats: Intended for non-commercial, personal, or open source use only

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Algolia account.
Create a new application and obtain your API keys.
Add your data to an index using the Algolia dashboard or API.
Integrate Algolia search into your app using a client library or widget.
Test your search and adjust relevance settings as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Algolia when you need fast, scalable, and easy-to-integrate search for web or mobile apps with minimal infrastructure management.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Algolia if you require advanced custom ranking, commercial use on the free tier, or need on-premises deployment.
avoidIf: large-scale commercial projects on free tier
entry.body: Algolia offers a generous free Community plan for personal and open source projects, making it easy to add powerful search to your app without managing infrastructure. The free tier is best for prototypes, personal sites, and open source, but commercial or high-traffic use requires a paid plan.

entry.title: anon.li Alias
entry.description: anon.li Alias provides free, privacy-focused email aliases for protecting your real email address online.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited free email aliases with forwarding and basic spam filtering.

useCases: Protecting your real email from spam
Signing up for online services anonymously
quickstartSteps: Visit https://anon.li/alias.
Enter your real email address to generate an alias.
Copy the generated alias and use it for signups.
Check your real inbox for forwarded messages.
Manage or delete aliases as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to shield your real email from spam or tracking during signups or online interactions.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require custom domains, advanced filtering, or a dedicated inbox for aliases.
entry.body: anon.li Alias offers free, privacy-first email aliases to help users protect their real email addresses from spam and tracking, with simple forwarding and no custom domain support.

entry.title: AnonAddy
entry.description: AnonAddy provides free, open-source email aliasing to protect your real email address from spam and tracking.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes unlimited aliases, 10MB bandwidth/month, and 2 recipients.
caveats: Custom domains and additional recipients require paid plan
Bandwidth overages suspend forwarding until next month

useCases: Protecting your real email from spam
Signing up for online services anonymously
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free AnonAddy account at https://anonaddy.com.
Verify your email address and log in to the dashboard.
Add your real email as a recipient.
Create a new alias or use a generated alias for signups.
Forward emails from aliases to your real inbox.
entry.whenToUse: Use AnonAddy when you want to shield your real email from spam or tracking while signing up for online services.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need high bandwidth, custom domains, or more than two recipients without upgrading to a paid plan.
entry.body: AnonAddy is an open-source email aliasing service that lets you create unlimited aliases to protect your real email address from spam and tracking. The free tier is suitable for individuals who want basic email privacy and are comfortable with bandwidth and recipient limits. It's easy to set up and ideal for students, indie users, or anyone needing simple, privacy-focused email forwarding.

entry.title: Antideo
entry.description: Antideo provides free APIs for IP geolocation, email validation, and phone number validation to help prevent fraud and improve data quality.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes limited daily API requests for IP, email, and phone validation.
caveats: No SLA or guaranteed uptime on free tier

useCases: Enrich user profiles with location data
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Antideo account at https://www.antideo.com.
Log in and navigate to the API dashboard.
Copy your API key from the dashboard.
Review the API documentation for endpoint details.
Integrate the API into your application using your API key.
entry.whenToUse: Use Antideo when you need basic, free validation of IP addresses, emails, or phone numbers to reduce spam or fraud.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high request volumes, guaranteed uptime, or advanced data enrichment beyond basic validation.
entry.body: Antideo offers free APIs for IP geolocation, email, and phone validation, making it easy to add basic fraud prevention and data quality checks to your apps.

entry.title: Appho.st
entry.description: Appho.st offers free static web hosting with custom domains and SSL support.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free static site hosting with custom domain and SSL.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Appho.st account.
Create a new site and choose a subdomain or add your custom domain.
Upload your static site files via the web interface.
Enable SSL for your domain.
Publish your site and verify it is live.
entry.whenToUse: Use Appho.st when you need to quickly host static sites with custom domains and SSL at no cost.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require backend processing, databases, or need to host large files or high-traffic sites.
entry.body: Appho.st provides a simple, always-free platform for hosting static websites with custom domains and SSL, ideal for personal and small project use.

entry.title: Appinvento
entry.description: Appinvento offers a no-code platform to build and deploy web and mobile applications quickly.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows building and publishing basic web and mobile apps with limited features.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Appinvento account.
Create a new project from the dashboard.
Use the drag-and-drop builder to design your app.
Configure basic settings and integrations.
Publish your app using the provided Appinvento domain.
entry.whenToUse: Use Appinvento when you need to quickly prototype or launch simple web or mobile apps without coding.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced customization, custom domains, or need to scale beyond basic features.
entry.body: Appinvento is a no-code platform for building and deploying web and mobile applications, ideal for rapid prototyping and simple app launches.

entry.title: Argos
entry.description: Argos provides visual regression testing for web applications with automated screenshot comparison and CI integration.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes 5,000 screenshots per month for public and private repositories.

useCases: Automated visual regression testing in CI pipelines
quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Argos account using GitHub or GitLab.
Connect your repository to Argos.
Install the Argos CLI in your project.
Configure your CI pipeline to upload screenshots with Argos CLI.
Review visual diffs in the Argos dashboard after each CI run.
entry.whenToUse: Use Argos when you need automated visual regression testing integrated with your CI workflow for web applications.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require testing for native mobile apps or need more than 5,000 screenshots per month without upgrading.
entry.body: Argos offers automated visual regression testing for web applications by comparing screenshots in your CI pipeline, helping teams catch unintended UI changes before deployment.

entry.title: Assertible
entry.description: Assertible provides automated API testing and monitoring with integrations for deployment pipelines and alerting.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 2 services, 1000 tests/month, and basic integrations.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Assertible account.
Create a new service and enter your API endpoint.
Define assertions to check API responses.
Set up test schedules and notification preferences.
Monitor results and receive alerts for failures.
entry.whenToUse: Use Assertible to automate API testing and uptime monitoring for small projects or when you need simple deployment pipeline integration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced test logic, high test volumes, or full team collaboration features.
entry.body: Assertible offers a simple, always-free plan for API monitoring and automated testing, making it suitable for small projects and basic uptime checks. The free tier is limited to 2 services and 1000 tests per month, with basic integrations and email alerts. It is easy to set up and integrates with deployment workflows, but lacks advanced scripting and team features found in higher tiers.

entry.title: Asurity
entry.description: Asurity provides free, privacy-focused static site hosting with custom domains and global CDN.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free static site hosting with custom domains, SSL, and CDN.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Asurity account.
Create a new site and choose a name.
Upload your static site files (HTML, CSS, JS).
Connect your custom domain if desired.
Publish your site and verify it is live.
entry.whenToUse: Use Asurity when you need simple, privacy-focused static site hosting with custom domains and global CDN for small projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require backend processing, large storage, high bandwidth, or advanced server-side features.
entry.body: Asurity offers free static site hosting with privacy in mind, supporting custom domains, SSL, and a global CDN for fast, secure delivery.

entry.title: AutoLocalise
entry.description: AutoLocalise automates translation and localization for apps, websites, and digital content using AI-powered workflows.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes limited monthly translations and basic project management features.
limits: Up to 5000 translated words per month
caveats: Advanced integrations and team features require paid plans
Some file formats may be restricted

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free AutoLocalise account.
Create a new localization project and upload your resource files.
Configure target languages and review AI-generated translations.
Download translated files or integrate with your deployment pipeline.
entry.whenToUse: Use AutoLocalise when you need to quickly localize digital products with minimal manual translation effort.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited translations, advanced team management, or support for rare file formats on the free tier.
entry.body: AutoLocalise provides an AI-driven platform for automating translation and localization tasks, ideal for small teams and projects needing quick, managed i18n workflows.

entry.title: Axiom
entry.description: Axiom provides scalable log and event analytics with real-time querying and visualization for observability and monitoring.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 500,000 events/day and 7-day data retention.
caveats: Data older than 7 days is deleted

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Axiom account.
Create a new dataset for your logs.
Install and configure an Axiom ingestion agent or use API to send logs.
Verify data is arriving in your dataset.
Build queries and dashboards to visualize your data.
entry.whenToUse: Use Axiom for aggregating and analyzing logs or events from apps or infrastructure with fast search and visualization needs.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-term log retention, advanced enterprise features, or expect to exceed free event limits regularly.
entry.body: Axiom offers a generous free tier for log and event analytics, ideal for small projects or teams needing fast, flexible observability tooling.

entry.title: Azure Advisor
entry.description: Azure Advisor provides personalized best practices and recommendations to optimize your Azure resources for cost, security, and performance.

freeTierDetails.summary: Azure Advisor is available at no additional cost for all Azure customers.
caveats: Some advanced recommendations may require enabling additional Azure services

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Navigate to 'Advisor' from the left-hand menu.
Review personalized recommendations across cost, security, reliability, and performance.
Apply suggested actions to optimize your resources.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Advisor when you want automated, actionable recommendations to optimize your Azure environment for cost, security, and performance.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need optimization for non-Azure environments or require highly customized, non-standard recommendations.
entry.body: Azure Advisor offers actionable recommendations to help you optimize your Azure resources for cost, security, performance, and reliability, at no additional charge.

entry.title: Azure Anomaly Detector
entry.description: Detects anomalies in time-series data using AI-powered models with simple API integration.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 20,000 transactions per month.
caveats: API usage only; no on-prem deployment
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if monthly transaction limit is exceeded

useCases: Monitor IoT sensor data for anomalies
Identify unexpected trends in business metrics
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal and create an Anomaly Detector resource.
Obtain your API key and endpoint from the resource overview.
Install the Azure Anomaly Detector SDK or use REST API.
Send time-series data to the API endpoint for anomaly detection.
Review results and integrate anomaly alerts into your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to detect anomalies in time-series data without building custom ML models.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require on-premises deployment or need to analyze non-time-series data.
entry.body: Azure Anomaly Detector provides scalable, AI-powered anomaly detection for time-series data via a simple API, ideal for monitoring and alerting use cases.

entry.title: Azure App Configuration
entry.description: Centralized service for managing application settings and feature flags across Azure environments.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1,000 requests per day and 10 MB storage.
caveats: Limited to 1 free store per subscription
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding limits incurs pay-as-you-go charges

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create a new App Configuration resource.
Add key-value pairs for your application settings.
Integrate the App Configuration SDK or REST API into your app.
Test retrieving configuration values from your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need centralized, scalable management of application settings or feature flags across multiple Azure services.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if your app has minimal configuration needs or you require on-premises configuration management.
entry.body: Azure App Configuration provides a centralized way to manage application settings and feature flags, supporting dynamic updates and integration with Azure services.

entry.title: Azure Arc
entry.description: Azure Arc enables management and governance of servers, Kubernetes clusters, and databases across on-premises, multi-cloud, and edge environments.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes resource organization, inventory, and some governance features for servers and Kubernetes clusters.
limits: No charge for resource organization and inventory
Advanced features (e.g., policy enforcement, security) may incur charges
caveats: Advanced management and security features are billed separately
Data transfer and connected Azure services may incur costs
billingRiskNotes: Enabling premium features or integrating with paid Azure services can generate charges

useCases: Centralized management of hybrid and multi-cloud resources
Inventory and monitoring of on-premises servers
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal and ensure you have an active subscription.
Register the Azure Arc resource provider in your subscription.
Download and install the Azure Connected Machine agent on your server or cluster.
Connect your on-premises or multi-cloud resources to Azure Arc following the guided setup.
Verify resource appearance and status in the Azure Portal.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Arc to manage, monitor, and govern resources across on-premises, multi-cloud, and edge environments from a single Azure control plane.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you only use native Azure resources or require advanced features without incurring additional costs.
entry.body: Azure Arc extends Azure management and governance to on-premises, multi-cloud, and edge resources, enabling unified operations and policy enforcement across diverse environments.

entry.title: Azure Bandwidth
entry.description: Azure Bandwidth provides outbound data transfer for Azure resources with a limited free monthly allowance.

freeTierDetails.summary: 5 GB outbound data transfer per month is free for all Azure accounts.
caveats: Applies only to outbound data; inbound is always free.
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding the free limit incurs automatic charges.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Azure account and verify your identity.
Create a resource (e.g., VM, App Service) in the Azure portal.
Configure your resource to send outbound data (e.g., serve a website or API).
Monitor bandwidth usage in the Azure portal to stay within the free tier.
Set up billing alerts to avoid unexpected charges.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need limited outbound data transfer for small apps, tests, or prototypes on Azure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if your workload regularly exceeds 5 GB/month outbound data or requires predictable, capped costs.
entry.body: Azure Bandwidth offers a small free outbound data transfer allowance, suitable for low-traffic apps, testing, and learning on Azure. Monitor usage to avoid overage charges.

entry.title: Azure Bastion
entry.description: Azure Bastion provides secure RDP and SSH connectivity to Azure VMs without exposing public IP addresses.

freeTierDetails.summary: Azure Bastion offers 750 hours and 50 GB outbound data transfer free per month for Standard SKU.
caveats: Only Standard SKU is included in free tier.
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue if usage exceeds free tier limits.

useCases: Secure VM access without public IPs
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create or select a Virtual Network.
Deploy Azure Bastion to the Virtual Network.
Assign Bastion access permissions.
Connect to your VM using Bastion via the Azure Portal.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Bastion when you need secure, browser-based RDP or SSH access to Azure VMs without exposing public IP addresses.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require access to on-premises or non-Azure VMs, or need advanced session recording features.
entry.body: Azure Bastion enables secure, seamless RDP and SSH connectivity to Azure VMs directly from the Azure Portal, eliminating the need for public IP addresses and reducing exposure to threats.

entry.title: Azure Blueprints
entry.description: Azure Blueprints enables repeatable, compliant environment setup by packaging policies, role assignments, and resources as templates.

freeTierDetails.summary: Azure Blueprints is available at no additional cost.
caveats: Charges apply for underlying Azure resources deployed
billingRiskNotes: Only pay for resources provisioned by blueprints

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Navigate to 'Blueprints' under 'Governance'.
Create a new blueprint definition or use a built-in sample.
Add artifacts such as policies, role assignments, and resource groups.
Publish and assign the blueprint to a subscription.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to automate and enforce consistent Azure environment configurations across subscriptions.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require cross-cloud governance or need advanced customization beyond Azure's built-in policy and resource options.
entry.body: Azure Blueprints lets you define and deploy repeatable sets of Azure resources, policies, and role assignments to ensure compliance and governance at scale.

entry.title: Azure Chaos Studio
entry.description: Azure Chaos Studio enables controlled fault injection experiments to improve application resilience and reliability on Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 5,000 minutes of experiment execution per month.
caveats: Some advanced faults may incur additional charges.
billingRiskNotes: Experiments stop when free tier is exhausted unless explicitly enabled for paid usage.

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal and create a Chaos Studio resource.
Register target resources (VMs, AKS, etc.) for chaos experiments.
Create an experiment by selecting faults and target resources.
Review and start the experiment to inject faults.
Monitor experiment results and analyze system behavior.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to validate your application's resilience to failures and improve reliability on Azure infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you lack Azure resources to test or need chaos engineering for non-Azure environments.
entry.body: Azure Chaos Studio provides a managed platform for running chaos engineering experiments, helping teams proactively identify and fix reliability issues in Azure-based applications.

entry.title: Azure Cost Management
entry.description: Azure Cost Management helps monitor, allocate, and optimize cloud spending across Azure resources and services.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for Azure customers to manage and analyze Azure resource costs.
limits: AWS cost management free for first 8 months
Some features may require additional Azure services
caveats: AWS and third-party cloud cost management is free for 8 months, then incurs charges
Some advanced analytics may require Power BI or other paid services

useCases: Allocate costs to projects or teams
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Navigate to 'Cost Management + Billing'.
Select 'Cost Management' and choose your subscription.
Review cost analysis and set up budgets or alerts as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to monitor, analyze, and control Azure cloud costs for your organization or projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require cost management for non-Azure clouds beyond the free AWS trial period or need deep multi-cloud analytics.
entry.body: Azure Cost Management provides free, built-in tools for tracking, analyzing, and optimizing Azure cloud spending, with basic AWS support for a limited time.

entry.title: Azure IoT Hub
entry.description: Azure IoT Hub is a managed service for secure, scalable device-to-cloud and cloud-to-device communication.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows up to 8,000 messages per day with basic device management.
caveats: No support for advanced security or scaling

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Create a new IoT Hub using the Free tier.
Register a device identity in the IoT Hub.
Download the device connection string.
Send test telemetry from your device or simulator.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to prototype or test IoT solutions with basic device connectivity and messaging at no cost.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced security, high message throughput, or production-grade SLAs.
entry.body: Azure IoT Hub provides a managed, scalable platform for connecting, monitoring, and managing IoT devices with secure cloud integration and basic messaging features.

entry.title: Azure Lab Services
entry.description: Azure Lab Services provides on-demand, managed lab environments for classrooms, training, and development scenarios.

freeTierDetails.summary: No always-free tier; costs depend on VM usage and configuration.
limits: Billing based on lab and VM size
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue as labs are used
No hard cap on usage

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal and create a Lab Services account.
Set up a new lab and configure the virtual machine template.
Publish the lab and invite users or students.
Monitor usage and manage lab schedules.
Shut down or delete labs to avoid unnecessary charges.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to provide managed, scalable lab environments for training, education, or testing with minimal infrastructure overhead.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require a free, always-on lab environment or need persistent VMs without ongoing usage costs.
entry.body: Azure Lab Services enables educators and teams to quickly set up, manage, and scale virtual lab environments for hands-on learning and training scenarios.

entry.title: Azure Lighthouse
entry.description: Azure Lighthouse enables cross-tenant management and automation for service providers and enterprises managing multiple Azure environments.

freeTierDetails.summary: Azure Lighthouse is free to use; you only pay for underlying Azure resources consumed.
caveats: No additional charge for Lighthouse itself
Resource consumption billed per standard Azure rates
billingRiskNotes: Only pay for Azure resources used, not for Lighthouse features

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal with an account that has the required permissions.
Create an Azure Lighthouse delegation ARM template or use the Azure portal to onboard a customer tenant.
Deploy the delegation template to the customer tenant or invite the customer to onboard.
Verify delegated access and manage resources across tenants from your service provider tenant.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Lighthouse when you need to manage resources across multiple Azure tenants with delegated access and automation.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you only manage a single Azure tenant or do not require cross-tenant automation and governance.
entry.body: Azure Lighthouse provides secure, scalable cross-tenant management, enabling service providers and enterprises to automate and govern resources across multiple Azure environments from a single control plane.

entry.title: Azure Managed Identities
entry.description: Azure Managed Identities provides automatic identity management for Azure resources to securely access other Azure services without credentials.

freeTierDetails.summary: Managed Identities are free with Azure resources; you only pay for the underlying resources.
caveats: Billing applies for Azure resources using identities
Premium Azure AD features may incur costs
billingRiskNotes: No direct cost for managed identities, but underlying resource usage may incur charges

useCases: Securely access Azure Key Vault from VMs
quickstartSteps: Create an Azure resource (e.g., VM, App Service) that supports managed identities.
Enable system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity for the resource.
Assign the managed identity necessary Azure role permissions (e.g., Key Vault Reader).
Update your application code to use Azure SDK authentication via managed identity.
Test access to the target Azure service without explicit credentials.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need secure, credential-free authentication for Azure resources accessing other Azure services.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require cross-cloud or on-premises authentication, or need identities outside Azure's managed scope.
entry.body: Azure Managed Identities lets Azure resources authenticate to supported services securely without managing credentials, reducing risk and simplifying access control.

entry.title: Azure Maps
entry.description: Azure Maps provides geospatial APIs for maps, search, routing, traffic, and weather data integration in web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: 1,000 map renders and 25,000 search transactions per month free.
caveats: Free tier applies to S0 pricing tier only.
billingRiskNotes: Usage beyond free limits incurs charges.
No hard cap; monitor usage to avoid unexpected costs.

useCases: Display interactive maps in web or mobile apps
Integrate real-time traffic or weather data
quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Azure account and create an Azure Maps resource.
Obtain your Azure Maps primary key from the Azure portal.
Install the Azure Maps SDK or use REST APIs in your application.
Configure authentication using your subscription key.
Start making API calls for maps, search, routing, or weather features.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Maps when you need scalable, cloud-based mapping, geolocation, or routing APIs integrated with other Azure services.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited free usage, offline maps, or need to avoid credit card registration for the free tier.
entry.body: Azure Maps offers robust APIs for mapping, geolocation, routing, and real-time data, with a generous free tier for developers and businesses building location-aware applications.

entry.title: Azure Network Watcher
entry.description: Azure Network Watcher provides network monitoring, diagnostic, and analytics tools for Azure resources.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes limited monitoring and diagnostic operations per month.
caveats: Some advanced features may incur charges.
Exceeding limits results in pay-as-you-go charges.
billingRiskNotes: Monitor usage to avoid unexpected charges if limits are exceeded.

useCases: Monitor network traffic and diagnose connectivity issues
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal and ensure you have an active subscription.
Navigate to 'Network Watcher' in the Azure portal.
Enable Network Watcher for your desired region.
Configure monitoring tools such as Connection Monitor or Packet Capture.
Review collected data and set up alerts as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to monitor, diagnose, or analyze network traffic and connectivity within Azure resources.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require monitoring for non-Azure environments or need unlimited diagnostics without risk of overage charges.
entry.body: Azure Network Watcher offers essential monitoring and diagnostic tools for Azure networks, with a free tier suitable for moderate usage and pay-as-you-go for higher volumes.

entry.title: Azure Open Datasets
entry.description: A collection of curated, open-access datasets hosted on Azure for machine learning, analytics, and research projects.

freeTierDetails.summary: Access to a wide range of open datasets at no cost.
limits: Standard Azure storage and compute charges apply if datasets are processed or copied
caveats: Downloading or copying data may incur Azure storage or bandwidth costs
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply only if you process or move data to your own Azure resources

quickstartSteps: Sign in to your Azure account or create a free account.
Navigate to the Azure Open Datasets portal.
Browse or search for relevant datasets.
Access datasets directly in Azure or link them to your compute resources.
Optionally, download or copy datasets to your own Azure storage for processing.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need reliable, curated public datasets for machine learning, analytics, or research within the Azure ecosystem.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require proprietary, private, or real-time datasets, or if you want to avoid any potential Azure storage or bandwidth costs.
entry.body: Azure Open Datasets provides free access to curated public datasets for machine learning, analytics, and research, with no direct dataset access fees.

entry.title: Azure Policy
entry.description: Azure Policy helps you enforce organizational standards and assess compliance at scale across Azure resources.

freeTierDetails.summary: Azure Policy is free for built-in policy definitions and assignments.
limits: Custom policy definitions may incur costs
caveats: Charges may apply for guest configuration and custom policies
Some advanced features require additional Azure services
billingRiskNotes: Custom or guest configuration policies may incur charges

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Navigate to 'Policy' under Azure services.
Assign a built-in policy to a subscription or resource group.
Review compliance results and remediate non-compliant resources.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Policy to enforce compliance and governance rules automatically across your Azure resources.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need policy enforcement outside Azure or require advanced custom policies without incurring extra costs.
entry.body: Azure Policy enables organizations to define, assign, and manage policies that enforce rules and effects for Azure resources, ensuring compliance and governance at scale.

entry.title: Azure Private Link
entry.description: Azure Private Link enables secure, private connectivity to Azure services over the Microsoft backbone network.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 50 hours of Private Link service and 50 GB data processed per month.
caveats: Overages billed per additional hour and GB.
Some Azure services may incur additional charges.
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free limits incurs pay-as-you-go charges.

useCases: Securely access Azure PaaS services from private networks
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Create or select a Virtual Network (VNet).
Navigate to Private Link Center and create a Private Endpoint.
Select the Azure resource to connect privately.
Approve the connection if required and test connectivity.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need private, secure connectivity to Azure services without exposing endpoints to the public internet.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if your workloads do not require private network isolation or if you need global, public access to services.
entry.body: Azure Private Link allows you to securely connect to Azure services over a private endpoint, reducing exposure to the public internet and improving compliance.

entry.title: Azure Purview
entry.description: Azure Purview is a unified data governance service for managing and discovering data across your organization.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1 vCore and 25 GB of data processed per month.
caveats: Additional usage billed per vCore-hour and per GB processed
Some features may require paid tier
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue if you exceed free tier limits

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create a new Azure Purview account.
Configure data sources and scan your data.
Review cataloged assets and metadata.
Set up access policies and governance rules.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Purview to organize, discover, and govern data assets across multiple sources in your organization.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need a free, unlimited data catalog or are not using Azure as your primary cloud platform.
entry.body: Azure Purview provides unified data governance, enabling organizations to catalog, discover, and manage data assets for compliance and analytics.

entry.title: Azure Resource Graph
entry.description: Azure Resource Graph enables fast, efficient exploration and query of Azure resources at scale across subscriptions.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free unlimited queries for resource exploration and management.

useCases: Inventory all Azure resources across subscriptions
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal with your account.
Navigate to Azure Resource Graph Explorer.
Select your subscriptions to query.
Write and run a Kusto Query Language (KQL) query to explore resources.
Review and export query results as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to query, inventory, or audit Azure resources across multiple subscriptions efficiently.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need to manage or query non-Azure resources, or require real-time event-based monitoring.
entry.body: Azure Resource Graph provides a scalable, always-free solution for querying and inventorying Azure resources, supporting governance, compliance, and management tasks across large environments.

entry.title: Azure Resource Manager
entry.description: Azure Resource Manager provides unified management and deployment of Azure resources using templates, policies, and access control.

freeTierDetails.summary: Azure Resource Manager usage is free; you only pay for the resources you deploy and manage.
caveats: Resource consumption incurs standard Azure charges
billingRiskNotes: Only pay for provisioned Azure resources

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal or CLI.
Create or select an Azure subscription.
Author an ARM template or use a sample template.
Deploy the template using Azure Portal, CLI, or PowerShell.
Monitor deployment status and manage resources via the portal.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to automate, manage, or standardize Azure resource deployments using templates and policies.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require multi-cloud management or need to manage non-Azure resources directly.
entry.body: Azure Resource Manager enables teams to automate and manage Azure resource deployments using declarative templates, policies, and access controls, supporting infrastructure-as-code best practices.

entry.title: Azure Traffic Manager
entry.description: Azure Traffic Manager is a DNS-based global traffic distribution service for high availability and performance.

freeTierDetails.summary: First 1 million DNS queries per month are free.
caveats: No advanced features in free tier
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue if query limit exceeded

useCases: Distribute user traffic across multiple Azure regions
Implement automatic failover for web applications
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal and create a Traffic Manager profile.
Select a routing method (e.g., Priority, Weighted, Performance).
Add endpoints for your application resources.
Configure DNS settings to point your domain to the Traffic Manager profile.
Monitor traffic and adjust routing as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to route global user traffic to multiple endpoints for high availability or performance.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require application-layer load balancing or need a free tier with no risk of overages.
entry.body: Azure Traffic Manager provides DNS-based global traffic distribution, enabling high availability and performance for applications deployed across multiple regions.

entry.title: Azure Virtual Networks
entry.description: Azure Virtual Networks provide secure, isolated, and scalable networking for Azure resources and services.

freeTierDetails.summary: Virtual Networks are free; charges apply for resources like VPN gateways, peering, or outbound data.
limits: Standard charges for VPN gateways, peering, NAT, and outbound data
caveats: Outbound data transfer, VPN gateways, and peering incur additional costs
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue if using VPN gateways, peering, or significant outbound data

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Navigate to 'Virtual Networks' and click 'Create'.
Enter a name, select a region, and define the address space.
Add one or more subnets as needed.
Review and create the virtual network.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need secure, private networking for Azure resources or to connect on-premises infrastructure to Azure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you only need public-facing resources or are not using Azure-hosted services.
entry.body: Azure Virtual Networks (VNets) enable secure, isolated, and scalable networking for Azure resources, supporting hybrid connectivity and granular network segmentation.

entry.title: Azure VPN Gateway
entry.description: Azure VPN Gateway enables secure site-to-site, point-to-site, and VNet-to-VNet connectivity across Azure and on-premises networks.

freeTierDetails.summary: No always-free tier; new users may access VPN Gateway via Azure free account credits.
caveats: No ongoing free tier; charges apply after credits expire
VPN Gateway incurs hourly and data transfer costs
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue automatically after credits are used or expire

useCases: Securely connect on-premises networks to Azure
Enable encrypted communication between Azure VNets
quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Azure account and activate your free $200 credit.
Navigate to the Azure Portal and search for 'VPN Gateway'.
Create a new Virtual Network and subnet for the gateway.
Deploy a VPN Gateway in your Virtual Network.
Configure site-to-site or point-to-site VPN connections as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure VPN Gateway to securely connect on-premises infrastructure or remote users to Azure virtual networks.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need a permanent free VPN solution or have low tolerance for unexpected network egress costs.
avoidIf: Low budget for ongoing network costs
entry.body: Azure VPN Gateway provides secure, scalable connectivity between Azure virtual networks and on-premises or remote environments, but does not offer an ongoing free tier.

entry.title: Beeceptor
entry.description: Beeceptor provides a free API endpoint for mocking, intercepting, and inspecting HTTP requests during development.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers one endpoint with limited request history and basic features.
caveats: No custom domain on free tier

useCases: Debugging HTTP requests without backend setup
quickstartSteps: Sign up at beeceptor.com with your email.
Create a new endpoint and name it.
Configure rules or mock responses as needed.
Point your application's HTTP requests to the Beeceptor endpoint.
Inspect requests and responses in the Beeceptor dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Beeceptor when you need to mock or inspect HTTP requests without setting up a backend or during early development.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high request volumes, custom domains, or advanced integrations; consider alternatives for production APIs.
entry.body: Beeceptor is a user-friendly API mocking and request inspection tool, ideal for development and testing scenarios with limited traffic or simple requirements.

entry.title: Bencher
entry.description: Bencher provides continuous benchmarking as a service to track and visualize software performance regressions.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes unlimited public projects and up to 3 private projects.
caveats: Private project limit applies to organization and personal accounts

useCases: Collaborate on open source performance metrics
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Bencher account at https://bencher.dev/.
Create a new project and select public or private visibility.
Integrate Bencher CLI into your CI workflow to submit benchmarks.
View and analyze performance trends on the Bencher dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Bencher to continuously monitor and visualize software performance, especially when integrating benchmarking into CI pipelines.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited private projects or need on-premise deployment; not ideal for highly sensitive or regulated data.
entry.body: Bencher is a benchmarking-as-a-service platform for tracking, visualizing, and sharing software performance metrics, with free unlimited public projects and limited private projects.

entry.title: Bitrise
entry.description: Bitrise is a CI/CD platform focused on automating mobile app builds, testing, and deployment.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 200 builds/month and 1 concurrent build for public or private mobile repositories.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Bitrise account.
Connect your mobile app repository (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or manual).
Select your project type (iOS, Android, etc.) and configure workflows.
Add build, test, and deploy steps as needed.
Trigger your first build and review results.
entry.whenToUse: Use Bitrise when you need automated CI/CD for mobile apps with minimal setup and strong integration with mobile tooling.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced customization, non-mobile workflows, or unlimited concurrent builds without cost.
entry.body: Bitrise provides a streamlined CI/CD solution tailored for mobile app development, offering easy setup, workflow automation, and integrations with popular mobile tools.

entry.title: Buddy
entry.description: Buddy is a CI/CD automation platform for building, testing, and deploying code with pipelines.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 5 projects, 120 pipeline runs/month, and 1 concurrent run.
caveats: Limited to public repositories for free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Buddy account.
Connect your GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository.
Create a new project and select your repository.
Set up a pipeline with build, test, and deploy actions.
Run the pipeline to automate your workflow.
entry.whenToUse: Use Buddy when you need simple, visual CI/CD pipelines for small projects or open source repositories.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Buddy if you require advanced enterprise features, unlimited private projects, or high concurrency for large teams.
entry.body: Buddy offers a user-friendly CI/CD platform with a generous free tier for automating builds, tests, and deployments, ideal for small teams and open source.

entry.title: BugBug
entry.description: BugBug is a no-code tool for automated browser testing of web applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan allows unlimited test cases and up to 1,000 test runs per month.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free BugBug account.
Create a new project and add your web application URL.
Record your first browser test using the visual editor.
Run the test manually or schedule automated runs.
Review test results and adjust steps as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use BugBug when you need simple, no-code browser test automation for web apps with minimal setup and maintenance.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require parallel test execution, advanced integrations, or support for mobile app testing.
entry.body: BugBug provides a free, no-code browser automation platform for web app testing, ideal for individuals and small teams needing basic automated tests without complex setup.

entry.title: Bugfender
entry.description: Bugfender provides remote logging and crash reporting for mobile and web applications to help developers diagnose issues in production.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes logging for up to 100,000 log lines per month and 2,000 devices.
caveats: No advanced features like user feedback or remote config

useCases: Monitoring app health in real time
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Bugfender account.
Create a new application in the Bugfender dashboard.
Integrate the Bugfender SDK into your app (iOS, Android, or web).
Initialize Bugfender with your app key.
Deploy your app and view logs in the Bugfender dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Bugfender when you need remote logging and crash reporting for mobile or web apps, especially for debugging issues in production.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need unlimited log retention, advanced analytics, or support for more than 2,000 devices on the free plan.
entry.body: Bugfender is a remote logging and crash reporting service for mobile and web apps, offering a generous free tier for small projects and debugging needs.

entry.title: Bugsink
entry.description: Bugsink provides automated error monitoring and reporting for web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 10,000 events per month with unlimited projects and users.

useCases: Monitor application errors in real time
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Bugsink account.
Create a new project in the dashboard.
Integrate the Bugsink SDK into your application.
Deploy your application and trigger an error to verify integration.
View error reports and set up alert notifications.
entry.whenToUse: Use Bugsink when you need simple, automated error monitoring for web or mobile apps with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced analytics, custom data retention, or integration with enterprise observability platforms.
entry.body: Bugsink offers a straightforward error monitoring solution with a generous free tier, making it ideal for small teams and projects needing basic bug tracking.

entry.title: Bump
entry.description: Bump provides disposable, privacy-focused email addresses for testing, signups, and protecting your inbox.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers limited disposable inboxes with basic features.
caveats: No custom domains on free tier

useCases: Signing up for services without sharing your real email
Avoiding spam in your main inbox
quickstartSteps: Go to https://bump.email/ and sign up for a free account.
Create a new disposable inbox from your dashboard.
Use the generated email address for signups or testing.
Check your Bump dashboard to view received emails.
Delete or rotate inboxes as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Bump when you need quick, disposable email addresses for testing, signups, or protecting your main inbox from spam.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Bump if you require persistent, long-term email storage or need custom domain support on the free tier.
entry.body: Bump is a privacy-focused disposable email service ideal for testing, signups, and protecting your real inbox from spam or unwanted messages.

entry.title: CacheFly
entry.description: CacheFly is a global content delivery network (CDN) offering fast, reliable content distribution with a generous free tier.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free CDN plan with 2 TB monthly bandwidth and global edge delivery.
caveats: No advanced analytics on free tier
billingRiskNotes: Overages beyond 2 TB are charged automatically

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free CacheFly account at the official signup page.
Add your website or application domain in the CacheFly dashboard.
Configure your origin server settings for content pull.
Update your DNS or application to point static asset URLs to CacheFly.
Verify content is being served via CacheFly CDN.
entry.whenToUse: Use CacheFly when you need a simple, high-bandwidth CDN for static content with generous free limits and global reach.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced analytics, custom edge logic, or need more than 2 TB/month bandwidth without incurring overage charges.
entry.body: CacheFly provides a generous always-free CDN tier suitable for static content delivery, with global edge locations and a 2 TB monthly bandwidth cap. It's ideal for indie developers, startups, and open source projects needing fast, reliable content distribution without upfront costs. Overages are billed automatically, so monitor usage if you expect high traffic.

entry.title: Calendarific

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows up to 1,000 API requests per month.

useCases: Integrate holiday data into HR or payroll systems
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Calendarific account.
Obtain your API key from the dashboard.
Read the API documentation for endpoint details.
Make a test API request using your key.
Integrate the API into your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use Calendarific when you need reliable, up-to-date public holiday data for multiple countries in your app or workflow.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited requests, advanced data, or need local holiday customizations not covered by the API.
entry.body: Calendarific offers a free API tier for accessing global public holiday data, suitable for low-traffic apps and prototypes needing holiday awareness.

entry.title: Calendly
entry.description: Calendly is an online scheduling tool that automates meeting bookings and calendar coordination.

freeTierDetails.summary: The Basic plan is free and allows for 1 calendar connection and unlimited one-on-one meetings.
caveats: No group events or collective scheduling

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Calendly account.
Connect your calendar (Google, Outlook, or others).
Set your availability preferences.
Create a one-on-one event type.
Share your scheduling link with others.
entry.whenToUse: Use Calendly to automate scheduling for one-on-one meetings and avoid back-and-forth emails.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced workflows, group scheduling, or deep integrations without upgrading to a paid plan.
entry.body: Calendly streamlines scheduling by letting others book time with you based on your real-time availability, reducing manual coordination.

entry.title: Canopy
entry.description: Canopy provides a unified API to access multiple financial data providers for banking, payments, and account aggregation.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 100 API calls per month.
caveats: Limited to sandbox and select endpoints

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Canopy account at https://www.canopyapi.co/.
Create a new application in the Canopy dashboard.
Obtain your API key from the dashboard.
Review the API documentation for endpoint details.
Make test API calls using your API key and sandbox data.
entry.whenToUse: Use Canopy when you need to quickly access multiple banking or payment data sources through a single unified API.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high-volume access, production usage without approval, or support for unsupported financial institutions.
entry.body: Canopy offers a unified API for accessing banking, payments, and account aggregation data, ideal for prototyping fintech applications with limited free usage.

entry.title: CatchJS.com
entry.description: CatchJS provides real-time JavaScript error monitoring for web applications with instant alerts and diagnostics.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes error tracking for one project with up to 1000 errors per month.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free CatchJS account.
Create a new project in the dashboard.
Copy the provided JavaScript snippet.
Paste the snippet into your website's HTML before the closing </body> tag.
Deploy your site and verify error reporting in the CatchJS dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use CatchJS when you need simple, real-time JavaScript error monitoring for a single web project with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require monitoring for multiple projects, advanced integrations, or higher error volume without upgrading.
entry.body: CatchJS offers a straightforward way to monitor and debug JavaScript errors in web applications, ideal for small projects or early-stage products.

entry.title: cdnjs
entry.description: cdnjs is a free, public CDN for hosting popular JavaScript and CSS libraries with fast global delivery.

freeTierDetails.summary: Completely free CDN for open-source libraries.
caveats: No support for private or proprietary assets

useCases: Quickly include popular JS/CSS libraries in web projects
Reduce server bandwidth by offloading static assets
Speed up website load times with global CDN
Simplify dependency management for static sites
quickstartSteps: Visit https://cdnjs.com/ and search for the desired library.
Select the version you need from the list.
Copy the provided CDN URL for the JS or CSS file.
Paste the URL into your HTML file's <script> or <link> tag.
Deploy your site; the asset will load from the CDN automatically.
entry.whenToUse: Use cdnjs to quickly add popular open-source JS or CSS libraries to your website without hosting them yourself.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid cdnjs if you need to host private, proprietary, or custom files, or require custom domain support.
entry.body: cdnjs is a free, public CDN maintained by Cloudflare for hosting and delivering popular open-source JavaScript and CSS libraries. It is ideal for quickly including widely-used libraries in web projects, reducing server bandwidth, and improving load times with minimal setup. However, it does not support private or custom files, nor does it offer custom domains or SLAs, making it best suited for open-source and non-critical production use.

entry.title: cDox
entry.description: cDox offers free, simple static site hosting with custom domains and SSL for small projects.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free static site hosting with custom domain and SSL.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free cDox account at https://cdox.ca.
Create a new site and choose a custom domain or use a cdox.ca subdomain.
Upload your static site files via the web interface.
Configure DNS settings if using a custom domain.
Verify SSL is enabled and your site is live.

entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need server-side logic, databases, or expect high traffic or large file hosting.
entry.body: cDox is a free static site hosting service offering custom domains and SSL, ideal for small personal or open source projects with simple requirements.

entry.title: CertKit

entry.title: CloudWatch
entry.description: AWS CloudWatch provides monitoring, logging, and observability for AWS resources and applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Includes free monthly usage for metrics, logs, and dashboards.
limits: 3 dashboards with up to 50 metrics each
caveats: Charges apply for usage above free limits.
Some features like Contributor Insights are not included.
billingRiskNotes: Overages can accumulate quickly if logs or metrics exceed free tier.

useCases: Monitor AWS EC2, Lambda, and other AWS services
Set up alarms for resource usage
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the AWS Management Console.
Navigate to CloudWatch from the AWS services menu.
Create a dashboard to visualize metrics.
Set up alarms for key metrics (e.g., CPU utilization).
Enable log collection for your AWS resources.
entry.whenToUse: Use CloudWatch to monitor AWS infrastructure, set up automated alarms, and collect logs for troubleshooting within AWS environments.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need to monitor non-AWS infrastructure or require advanced analytics not supported by CloudWatch.
entry.body: CloudWatch is AWS's native observability platform, offering integrated monitoring, logging, and alerting for AWS resources and applications with a generous free tier.

entry.title: Codacy
entry.description: Codacy provides automated code quality, security, and coverage analysis for repositories with integration to popular version control systems.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan for individuals and open source projects with unlimited public repositories.
caveats: Private repository support is limited on free tier

useCases: Automated code review for open source projects
Continuous code quality checks in CI/CD pipelines
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Codacy account using GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket.
Authorize Codacy to access your repositories.
Select a repository to add to Codacy.
Configure analysis settings and choose coding standards.
Trigger your first analysis and review the results.
entry.whenToUse: Use Codacy to automate code quality and security checks for public repositories or small private projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced features, support for many private repositories, or deep customization without upgrading to a paid plan.
avoidIf: projects needing extensive private repo support
entry.body: Codacy offers automated static code analysis and security scanning for developers, with a generous free tier for public and limited private repositories.

entry.title: CodSpeed
entry.description: CodSpeed provides automated benchmarking and performance regression detection for your CI pipelines.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes unlimited public repositories and up to 1 private repository.
caveats: Private repository support limited to one per account

Connect your GitHub or GitLab repository.
Install the CodSpeed agent in your CI pipeline.

Review performance results on the CodSpeed dashboard.

entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need support for multiple private repositories or advanced analytics without a paid plan.
entry.body: CodSpeed integrates with your CI to benchmark code and detect performance regressions, offering a free tier for public and one private repository.

entry.title: Wormhol.org
entry.description: Wormhol.org provides free, ephemeral, end-to-end encrypted file sharing with instant links and no account required.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free, anonymous file sharing with end-to-end encryption and automatic expiry.
limits: Links expire after 24 hours or one download
caveats: Files are deleted after expiry or download

quickstartSteps: Go to https://www.wormhol.org/ in your browser.
Drag and drop or select your file (up to 10 GB).
Wait for the upload and copy the generated link.
Share the link with your recipient.
Recipient downloads the file; link expires after download or 24 hours.
entry.whenToUse: Use Wormhol.org to quickly and securely share large files without creating an account or leaving files online permanently.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need persistent storage, file versioning, or sharing with multiple recipients over time.
entry.body: Wormhol.org enables fast, secure, and anonymous file sharing with automatic expiry, making it ideal for one-off transfers without persistent storage.

entry.title: Wormhole

Wait for the upload and encryption to complete.

Recipient downloads the file before the link expires (24 hours or 100 downloads).

entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Wormhole if you need persistent file storage, collaboration features, or transfers larger than 10GB.
entry.body: Wormhole is a privacy-focused, browser-based service for sending encrypted files up to 10GB with automatic link expiration and no account required.
